	<div class="dsRightСol dsColorFrLoV">
		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00011" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00011"><span class="tt kwbu">Lone</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Operative</span></span>, <span class="tooltip00085" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00085"><span class="tt kwbu">Stealth</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Grudge-fuelled Fortitude:</b> The first time this model is destroyed, at the end of the phase, roll one D6: on a 2+, set this model back up on the battlefield as close as possible to where it was destroyed and not within <span class="tooltip00031" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00031"><span class="tt kwbu">Engagement</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Range</span></span> of one or more enemy units, with its full wounds remaining.<div class="dsLineHor"></div><b>Unhinged Vengeance:</b> In your opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, each time an enemy unit has shot, if this model lost one or more wounds as a result of those attacks, this model can make a Vengeance move. To do so, roll one D6 and add 2 to the result: it moves a number of inches up to this result, but it must end that move as close as possible to the closest enemy unit (excluding <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Aircraft"><span class="kwb">AIRCRAFT</span></a>). When doing so, it can be moved within <span class="tooltip00031 tooltipstered" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00031" style="opacity: 1;"><span class="tt kwbu">Engagement</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Range</span></span> of that enemy unit. This model cannot make a Vengeance move while it is <span class="tooltip00027" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00027"><span class="tt kwbu">Battle-shocked</span></span> or within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, and can only make one Vengeance move per phase.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>1 Buri Aegnirssen – <span class="kwb">EPIC</span> <span class="kwb">HERO</span></b></li></ul><b>This model is equipped with:</b> Autoch-pattern bolt pistol; Bane.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>1 model</td><td><div class="PriceTag">95</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div>
	</div>
</div>

	</div>
	<div class="dsRightСol dsColorFrLoV">
		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00010" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00010"><span class="tt kwbu">Leader</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Computational Mastermind:</b> At the end of your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#COMMAND-PHASE">Command phase</a>, before you determine if units from your army have <span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Hostile</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Acquisition</span></span> or <span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Fortify</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Takeover</span></span>, for each <span class="tooltip00143" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00143"><span class="tt kwbu">objective</span> <span class="tt kwbu">marker</span></span> you control that has one or more models with this ability within range of it, you can spend 1YP or gain 1YP.<div class="dsLineHor"></div><b>Predictive Guidance:</b> Once per <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#The-Battle-Round">battle round</a>, this model can use this ability. If it does, you can target this unit with the <span class="tooltip00041" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00041"><span class="tt kwbu">Fire</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Overwatch</span></span> or <span class="tooltip00048" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00048"><span class="tt kwbu">Heroic</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Intervention</span></span> Stratagem for 0CP.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>1 Memnyr Strategist</b></li></ul><b>This model is equipped with:</b> Autoch-pattern bolt pistol; close combat weapon.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>1 model</td><td><div class="PriceTag">45</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">LEADER</div>

class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">WARGEAR OPTIONS</div>
<ul><li>Any number of Hearthkyn Warriors can each have their Autoch-pattern bolter replaced with 1 ion blaster.</li><li>Up to 2 Hearthkyn Warriors can each have their  Autoch-pattern bolter or ion blaster replaced with one of the following (duplicates are not allowed):<ul style="list-style-type:circle"><li>1 HYLas auto rifle</li><li>1 HYLas rotary cannon</li><li>1 L7 missile launcher</li><li>1 EtaCarn plasma beamer</li><li>1 magna-rail rifle</li></ul></li><li>Up to 2 Hearthkyn Warriors can each have their Autoch-pattern bolter or ion blaster replaced with 1 plasma knife.</li><li>The Theyn’s Autoch-pattern bolter can be replaced with one of the following:<ul style="list-style-type:circle"><li>1 ion blaster</li><li>1 Theyn’s pistol</li></ul></li><li>The Theyn can be equipped with 1 Theyn’s melee weapon.</li></ul><br>
	</div>
	<div class="dsRightСol dsColorFrLoV">
		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00137" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00137"><span class="tt kwbu">Feel</span> <span class="tt kwbu">No</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Pain</span> <span class="tt kwbu">6+</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Luck Has. Need Keeps. Toil Earns:</b> At the end of your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#COMMAND-PHASE">Command phase</a>, if this unit is within range of an <span class="tooltip00143" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00143"><span class="tt kwbu">objective</span> <span class="tt kwbu">marker</span></span> you control, that objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective markers greater than yours at the end of a phase.<div class="dsLineHor"></div><b>Panspectral Scanning:</b> Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack, re-roll a <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span> of 1.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">WARGEAR ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Weavefield Crest:</b> Models in the bearer’s unit have a 5+ <span class="tooltip00044" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00044"><span class="tt kwbu">invulnerable</span> <span class="tt kwbu">save</span></span>.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>1 Theyn</b></li></ul><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>9 Hearthkyn Warriors</b></li></ul><b>The Theyn is equipped with:</b> Autoch-pattern bolt pistol; Autoch-pattern bolter; close combat weapon; weavefield crest.<br><br><b>Every Hearthkyn Warrior is equipped with:</b> Autoch-pattern bolt pistol; Autoch-pattern bolter; close combat weapon.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>10 models</td><td><div class="PriceTag">100</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div>
	</div>

<a name="Dedicated-Transports"></a><h2 class="outline_header">Dedicated Transports</h2>
<div><br></div>
<a name="Kapricus-Carrier"></a><div class="dsOuterFrame datasheet pagebreak clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="position: relative;"><div class="dsBannerWrap"><div class="dsIconsNarrow"><div class="dsIconsRoundPad dsColorBanLoV"><div class="picLegend tooltip_" title="A variant of the adaptable Kapricus design, the Carrier provides elite teams of Hernkyn Yaegirs with the means to penetrate enemy lines at speed. The transport’s quad magna-coils grant it the manoeuvrability to traverse almost any frontier terrain. At the same time, its banks of scanner uplinks enable it to lay down withering hails of accurate, suppressive fire to purge claim sites."></div></div><div class="dsIconsRoundPad dsColorBanLoV"><a target="_blank" href="https://www.google.com/search?tbm=isch&amp;q=Warhammer%2040000%20Leagues%20of%20Votann%20Kapricus%20Carrier%20miniature"><div title="Search for model’s image on the Internet" class="picSearch tooltip_"></div></a></div></div><div class="dsPicNarrow"><img class="logo3 tooltip_" title="Faction Pack. 		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00006" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00006"><span class="tt kwbu">Deadly</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Demise</span> <span class="tt kwbu">1</span></span>, <span class="tooltip00155" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00155"><span class="tt kwbu">Firing</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Deck</span> <span class="tt kwbu">5</span></span>, <span class="tooltip00156" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00156"><span class="tt kwbu">Scouts</span> <span class="tt kwbu">9"</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Scanner Uplinks:</b> In your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, after this model has shot, select one enemy unit (excluding <span class="kwb">MONSTERS</span> and <span class="kwb">VEHICLES</span>) hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the start of your next turn, that enemy unit is suppressed. While a unit is suppressed, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">WARGEAR ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Smoke Launcher:</b> The bearer has the <span class="kwb">SMOKE</span> keyword.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>1 Kapricus Carrier</b></li></ul><b>This model is equipped with:</b> magna-coil autocannon; twin magna-coil autocannon; armoured hull.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>1 model</td><td><div class="PriceTag">75</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">TRANSPORT</div>
<div class="dsAbility">This model has a <span class="tooltip00158" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00158"><span class="tt kwbu">transport</span> <span class="tt kwbu">capacity</span></span> of 5 <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Hernkyn-Yaegirs"><span class="kwb kwbo">HERNKYN</span> <span class="kwb kwbo">YAEGIR</span></a> models.<br><br>At the start of the Declare Battle Formations step, you can select one <span class="kwb">HERNKYN</span> <span class="kwb">YAEGIR</span> unit from your army that has not been split. If you do, that unit is split into two units, each containing as equal a number of models as possible (when splitting a unit in this way, make a note of which models form each of the two new units). One of these units must start the battle <span class="tooltip00070" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00070"><span class="tt kwbu">embarked</span></span> within this <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span>; the other can start the battle embarked within another <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span>, or it can be deployed as a separate unit.</div>
	</div>

<ul><li>This model’s HYLas beam cannon can be replaced with one of the following:<ul style="list-style-type:circle"><li>1 L7 missile launcher and 1 Sagitaur missile launcher</li><li>1 MATR autocannon</li></ul></li></ul><br>

	<div class="dsRightСol dsColorFrLoV">
		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00006" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00006"><span class="tt kwbu">Deadly</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Demise</span> <span class="tt kwbu">1</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Blistering Advance:</b> Units can <span class="tooltip00002" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00002"><span class="tt kwbu">disembark</span></span> from this <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Transports"><span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span></a> after it has <span class="tooltip00023" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00023"><span class="tt kwbu">Advanced</span></span>. Units that do so count as having made a Normal move that phase, and cannot <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#CHARGE-PHASE">declare a charge</a> in the same turn, but can otherwise act normally in the remainder of the turn.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>1 Sagitaur</b></li></ul><b>This model is equipped with:</b> HYLas beam cannon; twin bolt cannon; armoured wheels.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>1 model</td><td><div class="PriceTag">90</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">TRANSPORT</div>
<div class="dsAbility">This model has a <span class="tooltip00158" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00158"><span class="tt kwbu">transport</span> <span class="tt kwbu">capacity</span></span> of 6 <span class="tooltip00009" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00009" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00009"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">INFANTRY</span></span> models. It cannot transport <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Cthonian-Earthshakers"><span class="kwb kwbo">ARTILLERY</span></a>, <span class="tooltipExoarmour" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_contentExoarmour" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_contentExoarmour"><span class="kwb kwbu">EXOARMOUR</span></span>, <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Brôkhyr-Thunderkyn"><span class="kwb kwbo">EXOFRAME</span></a> or <span class="tooltip00061" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00061" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00061"><span class="kwb kwbu">IRONKIN</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">STEELJACKS</span></span> models.<br><br>At the start of the Declare Battle Formations step, you can select one <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Hearthkyn-Warriors"><span class="kwb kwbo">HEARTHKYN</span> <span class="kwb kwbo">WARRIORS</span></a> unit from your army that has not been split. If you do, that unit is split into two units, each containing as equal a number of models as possible (when splitting a unit in this way, make a note of which models form each of the two new units). One of these units must start the battle <span class="tooltip00070" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00070"><span class="tt kwbu">embarked</span></span> within this <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span>; the other can start the battle embarked within another <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span>, or it can be deployed as a separate unit.</div>
	</div>
</div>

OPTIONS</div>
<ul><li>All models in this unit can each have their heavy plasma axe replaced with 1 concussion maul.</li><li>For every 5 models in this unit, 1 model that is not equipped with a mole grenade launcher can have its heavy plasma axe or concussion maul replaced with 1 twin concussion gauntlet.</li><li>For every 5 models in this unit, 1 model that is not equipped with a twin concussion gauntlet or a mole grenade launcher can be equipped with 1 mole grenade launcher.*</li></ul><br><div class="dsOptionsComment"><b>* Designer’s Note:</b> If a Cthonian Beserk is equipped with a mole grenade launcher, it will be accompanied by an L-COG unit: both models should be placed on the same base and together they act as a single model for all rules purposes.</div>
	</div>
	<div class="dsRightСol dsColorFrLoV">
		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00137" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00137"><span class="tt kwbu">Feel</span> <span class="tt kwbu">No</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Pain</span> <span class="tt kwbu">4+</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Cyberstimms:</b> Each time a model in this unit is destroyed by a melee attack, if that model has not fought this phase, roll one D6, adding 1 to the result if units from your army have <span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Fortify</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Takeover</span></span>: on a 4+, do not remove it from play. The destroyed model can fight after the attacking unit has finished making its attacks, and is then removed from play.<div class="dsLineHor"></div><b>Subterranean Explosives:</b> In your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, after this unit has shot, select one enemy unit (excluding <span class="kwb">MONSTERS</span> and <span class="kwb">VEHICLES</span>) hit by one or more of those attacks made with a mole grenade launcher. Until the start of your next Shooting phase, that enemy unit cannot be targeted with the <span class="tooltip00041" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00041"><span class="tt kwbu">Fire</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Overwatch</span></span> Stratagem.<br><br><b>Designer’s Note:</b> <i>We recommend placing a Mole Grenade token next to that enemy unit as a reminder.</i></div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>

	<div class="dsRightСol dsColorFrLoV">
		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Destabilising Quakes:</b> In your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, after this unit has shot, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks made with its tremor shells. That unit must take a <span class="tooltip00027" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00027"><span class="tt kwbu">Battle-shock</span> <span class="tt kwbu">test</span></span>, subtracting 1 from the result.<div class="dsLineHor"></div><b>Geomantic Hunters:</b> Up to twice per battle, in your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, when this unit is selected to shoot, it can use this ability. If it does, until the end of the phase, each time a model in this unit makes an attack with its breacher ordnance, you can re-roll the <span class="tooltip00021" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00021"><span class="tt kwbu">Wound</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>.<br><br><b>Designer’s Note:</b> <i>Place two breacher ordnance tokens next to the unit, removing one each time this unit uses this ability.</i></div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>2 Cthonian Earthshakers</b></li></ul><b>Every model is equipped with:</b> 2 Autoch-pattern bolt pistols, breacher ordnance; plasma picks.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>2 models</td><td><div class="PriceTag">110</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div>
	</div>

<ul><li>All models in this unit can each have their EtaCarn plasma gun replaced with 1 volkanite disintegrator.</li><li>All models in this unit can each have their concussion gauntlet replaced with 1 plasma blade gauntlet.</li><li>The Hesyr’s concussion gauntlet or plasma blade gauntlet can be replaced with 1 graviton hammer.</li><li>The Hesyr’s weavefield crest can be replaced with 1 teleport crest.</li></ul><br>

		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00006" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00006"><span class="tt kwbu">Deadly</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Demise</span> <span class="tt kwbu">D6</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>MultiCOG Targeting:</b> Each time this model makes a ranged attack, you can ignore any or all modifiers to the following: that attack’s Ballistic Skill characteristic; the <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">WARGEAR ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Panspectral Scanner:</b> Each time a model in the bearer’s unit makes a ranged attack, re-roll a <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span> of 1.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>1 Hekaton Land Fortress</b></li></ul><b>This model is equipped with:</b> cyclic ion cannon; MATR autocannon; 2 twin bolt cannons; armoured wheels; pan spectral scanner.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>1 model</td><td><div class="PriceTag">240</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV"><span class="dsSkull2"></span>DAMAGED: 1-5 WOUNDS REMAINING</div>
<div class="dsAbility">While this model has 1-5 wounds remaining, each time this model makes an attack, subtract 1 from the <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">TRANSPORT</div>

It cannot transport <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Cthonian-Earthshakers"><span class="kwb kwbo">ARTILLERY</span></a> models.</div>

OPTIONS</div>
<ul><li>For every 3 models in this unit, 1 model can be equipped with one of the following::<ul style="list-style-type:circle"><li>1 HYLas rotary cannon</li><li>1 ion beamer</li></ul></li><li>1 model that is not equipped with either a HYLas rotary cannon or an ion beamer can be equipped with 1 multiwave comms array.*</li><li>1 model that is not equipped with either a HYLas rotary cannon or an ion beamer can be equipped with 1 panspectral scanner.*</li><li>1 model that is not equipped with either a HYLas rotary cannon or an ion beamer can be equipped with 1 rollbar searchlight.*</li></ul><br><div class="dsOptionsComment">* A model can only take one of these options, and cannot be equipped with a HYLas rotary cannon or an ion beamer.</div>

		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">CORE: <b><span class="tooltip00156" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00156"><span class="tt kwbu">Scouts</span> <span class="tt kwbu">9"</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Outflanking Mag-riders:</b> At the end of your opponent’s turn, if this unit is wholly within 9" of one or more battlefield edges and not within <span class="tooltip00031" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00031"><span class="tt kwbu">Engagement</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Range</span></span> of one or more enemy units, you can remove it from the battlefield and place it into <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Strategic-Reserves">Strategic Reserves</a>.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">WARGEAR ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Multiwave Comms Array:</b> Each time you target the bearer’s unit with a Stratagem, roll one D6: on a 5+, you gain 1CP.</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Panspectral Scanner:</b> Each time a model in the bearer’s unit makes a ranged attack, re-roll a <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span> of 1.</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Rollbar Searchlight:</b> Each time a model in the bearer’s unit makes a ranged attack, you can ignore any or all modifiers to the <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>3-6 Hernkyn Pioneers</b></li></ul><b>Every model is equipped with:</b> bolt revolver; bolt shotgun; magna-coil autocannon; plasma knife.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>3 models</td><td><div class="PriceTag">80</div></td></tr><tr><td>6 models</td><td><div class="PriceTag">160</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

		<div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility">FACTION: <b><span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Prioritised</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Efficiency</span></span></b></div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Purge Response:</b> Each time you target this unit with the <span class="tooltip00041" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00041"><span class="tt kwbu">Fire</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Overwatch</span></span> Stratagem, hits are scored on unmodified <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">rolls</span></span> of 5+ while resolving that Stratagem. If units from your army have <span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Fortify</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Takeover</span></span>, hits are scored on unmodified Hit rolls of 4+ while resolving that Stratagem instead.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">WARGEAR ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Preymark Crest:</b> Each time a model in the bearer’s unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit within range of one or more <span class="tooltip00143" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00143"><span class="tt kwbu">objective</span> <span class="tt kwbu">markers</span></span>, on a <span class="tooltip00021" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00021"><span class="tt kwbu">Critical</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Wound</span></span>, that attack has the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><span class="tooltip00050" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00050"><span class="tt kwbu">[PRECISION]</span></span></span> ability.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>1 Steeljack Theyn</b></li></ul><ul class="dsUl"><li><b>2-5 Ironkin Steeljacks</b></li></ul><b>The Steeljack Theyn is equipped with:</b> heavy volkanite disintegrator; plasma knife; preymark crest.<br><br><b>Every Ironkin Steeljack is equipped with:</b> heavy volkanite disintegrator; plasma knife.</div><div class="dsAbility"><table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="2"><tbody><tr><td>3 models</td><td><div class="PriceTag">85</div></td></tr><tr><td>6 models</td><td><div class="PriceTag">170</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

If it does, until the end of the turn, this unit is not eligible to <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#CHARGE-PHASE">declare a charge</a>.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">WARGEAR ABILITIES</div><div class="dsAbility"><b>Smoke Launcher:</b> The bearer has the <span class="kwb">SMOKE</span> keyword.</div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">UNIT COMPOSITION</div>

	<div class="dsRightСol dsColorFrLoV">
		<div><div class="dsHeader dsColorBgLoV">DETACHMENT ABILITY</div>
<div class="dsAbility"><div class="s10EnhWrap clFl clFl LVLV LVHE" style=""><div><span class="tooltip00088" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00088"><span class="tt kwbu">Methodical</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Annihilation</span></span></div></div><div class="s10EnhWrap clFl clFl LVLV LVNO" style="display: none;"><div><span class="tooltip00089" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00089"><span class="tt kwbu">Martial</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Leverage</span></span></div></div><div class="s10EnhWrap clFl clFl LVLV LVPP" style="display: none;"><div><span class="tooltip00082" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00082"><span class="tt kwbu">Assailed</span> <span class="tt kwbu">From</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Every</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Angle</span></span></div></div><div class="s10EnhWrap clFl clFl LVLV LVMO" style="display: none;"><div><span class="tooltip00093" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00093"><span class="tt kwbu">Ruthless</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Reinvestment</span></span></div></div></div></div>
	</div>
</div></div>
<!--noindex--><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00001"><div class="tooltip_header">Destroyed</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Destroyed"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
Throughout a battle, models will suffer damage and be destroyed. When a model is destroyed, it is removed from the battlefield. When every model in a unit has been destroyed, that unit is destroyed.
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00002"><div class="tooltip_header">Disembark</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Disembark"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
If a unit from your army starts your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#MOVEMENT-PHASE">Movement phase</a> embarked within a <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model, that unit can disembark in that phase.<br><br>When a unit disembarks from a <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model, set it up on the battlefield so that it is wholly within 3" of that <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model and not within <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range">Engagement Range</a> of any enemy models. If, for any reason, a disembarking model cannot be set up, that model’s unit cannot disembark.<br><br>Units that disembark from a <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model that either <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Remain-Stationary">Remained Stationary</a> this phase or has not yet made a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Normal-Moves">Normal</a>, <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Advance-Moves">Advance</a> or <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Fall-Back-Moves">Fall Back move</a> this phase can then act normally (make a Normal move, Advance, shoot, declare a charge, fight, etc.) in the remainder of the turn. Such a disembarking unit cannot choose to <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Remain-Stationary">Remain Stationary</a>.<br><br>Units that disembark from a <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model that made a Normal move this phase count as having made a Normal move themselves; they cannot move further during this phase. Such a unit also cannot declare a charge in the same turn, but can otherwise act normally in the remainder of the turn.<br><br>Units cannot disembark from a <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model that either <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Advance-Moves">Advanced</a> or <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Fall-Back-Moves">Fell Back</a> this turn.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>Units that start your Movement phase embarked within a <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> can disembark this phase, provided their <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> has not Advanced or Fallen Back.</li><li>If a unit disembarks before its <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> moves, it can act normally.</li><li>If a unit disembarks after its <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> moves, it cannot move or charge this turn, but can otherwise act normally.</li><li>Disembarking units must be set up wholly within 3" of their <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> and not within Engagement Range of any enemy models (or the unit cannot disembark).</li><li>Units that disembark this turn cannot Remain Stationary.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00003"><div class="tooltip_header">Hazardous</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Hazardous"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Weapons powered by unstable and dangerous energy sources pose a substantial risk to the wielder every time they are used.</p>Weapons with <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[HAZARDOUS]</span> in their profile are known as Hazardous weapons. Each time a unit is selected to shoot or fight, after that unit has resolved all of its attacks, for each Hazardous weapon that targets were selected for when resolving those attacks, that unit must take one Hazardous test. To do so, roll one D6: on a 1, that test is failed. For each failed test you must resolve the following sequence (resolve each failed test one at a time): <br><ul><li>If possible, select one model in that unit that has lost one or more wounds and is equipped with one or more Hazardous weapons. </li><li>Otherwise, if possible, select one model in that unit (excluding <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> models) equipped with one or more Hazardous weapons. </li><li>Otherwise, select one <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model in that unit equipped with one or more Hazardous weapons.</li></ul>If a model was selected, that unit suffers 3 <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Mortal-Wounds">mortal wounds</a> and when allocating those mortal wounds, they must be allocated to the selected model.<br>If a unit from a player’s army is selected as the target of the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/test_/#Стратегема">Fire Overwatch</a> Stratagem in their opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#CHARGE-PHASE">Charge phase</a>, any mortal wounds inflicted by Hazardous tests are allocated after the charging unit has ended its Charge move.<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> A unit of five models make five attacks with ranged weapons with the <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[HAZARDOUS]</span> ability. After the unit has finished shooting, its controlling player rolls five D6. One of the results is a 1, and so one of those models suffers 3 mortal wounds.</div><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>After a unit shoots or fights, roll one Hazardous test (one D6) for each Hazardous weapon used. For each 1, a model equipped with a hazardous weapon suffers 3 mortal wounds, that must be allocated to the selected model.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00004"><div class="tooltip_header">Devastating Wounds</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Devastating-Wounds"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
Weapons with <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[DEVASTATING WOUNDS]</span> in their profile are known as Devastating Wounds weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if that attack scores a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Wound-Roll">Critical Wound</a>, no <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#4.-Saving-Throw">saving throw</a> of any kind can be made against that attack (including <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Invulnerable-Saves">invulnerable saving throws</a>). Such attacks are only allocated to models after all other attacks made by the attacking unit have been allocated and resolved. After that attack is allocated and after any modifiers are applied, it inflicts a number of <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Mortal-Wounds">mortal wounds</a> on the target equal to the Damage characteristic of that attack, instead of inflicting damage normally.<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> An attack made with a Devastating Wounds weapon with a Damage characteristic of 2 scores a Critical Wound. Instead of allocating the attack and making saving throws normally, the target suffers 2 mortal wounds.</div><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>A Critical Wound inflicts mortal wounds equal to the weapon’s Damage characteristic, instead of any normal damage.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00005"><div class="tooltip_header">Mortal Wounds</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Mortal-Wounds"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Some attacks are so powerful that no armour or force field can withstand their fury.</p>Some rules inflict mortal wounds on units. Each time mortal wounds are inflicted on a unit, each of those mortal wounds inflicts one point of damage to that unit, and they are always applied one at a time. Each mortal wound is allocated to a model in the same manner as allocating an attack. Excess damage from mortal wounds is not lost if the damage can be allocated to another model. Instead, keep allocating damage to another model in the target unit until either all the damage has been allocated or the target unit is destroyed.<br><br>If an attack inflicts mortal wounds in addition to any normal damage, do not make a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Wound-Roll">Wound roll</a> or <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#4.-Saving-Throw">saving throw</a> (including <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Invulnerable-Saves">invulnerable saving throws</a>) against those mortal wounds. If those mortal wounds are inflicted by an attack that has the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Precision">[PRECISION]</a></span> ability, the attacking model’s controlling player can allocate those mortal wounds to a <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model in that unit.<br><br>If mortal wounds are being inflicted as a result of the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Hazardous">[HAZARDOUS]</a></span> ability or by an attack with the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Devastating-Wounds">[DEVASTATING WOUNDS]</a></span> ability that scored a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Wound-Roll">Critical Wound</a>, each time those mortal wounds are allocated to a model, if that model is destroyed as a result of those mortal wounds, the remaining mortal wounds from that attack are lost, just as with a normal attack.<br><br>If, when a unit is selected to shoot or fight, one or more of its attacks can inflict mortal wounds on the target, resolve any normal damage inflicted by the attacking unit’s attacks on that target before inflicting any mortal wounds on that target. If an attack inflicts mortal wounds in addition to any normal damage, but the normal damage is subsequently saved, the target unit still suffers those mortal wounds, as described above.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>Each mortal wound inflicted on a unit causes one model in that unit to lose one wound.</li><li>Unless the source of the mortal wounds is the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><span class="tooltip00003" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00003"><span class="tt kwbu">[HAZARDOUS]</span></span></span> ability or an attack with the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><span class="tooltip00004" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00004"><span class="tt kwbu">[DEVASTATING</span> <span class="tt kwbu">WOUNDS]</span></span></span> ability, damage from mortal wounds is not lost if it can be allocated to another model. </li><li>No saving throws can be made against mortal wounds.</li><li>Mortal wounds inflicted by attacks always apply after any normal damage, even if that damage was saved.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00006"><div class="Corner33 abWrap BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="abFlex"><div class="abDiamondWrap"><div class="abDiamond"><div class="abIcon"></div></div></div><div class="abLine"></div><div class="abNameWrap"><div class="abName">DEADLY DEMISE</div></div></div><p style="padding-bottom:8px" class="ShowFluff stratLegend2">From detonating ammo stores to corrosive innards or frenzied death throes, some targets are deadly even in defeat.</p>Some models have ‘Deadly Demise x’ listed in their abilities. When such a model is <span class="tooltip00001" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00001"><span class="tt kwbu">destroyed</span></span>, roll one D6 before removing it from play (if such a model is a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Transports"><span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span></a>, roll before any embarked models <span class="tooltip00002" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00002"><span class="tt kwbu">disembark</span></span>). On a 6, each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of <span class="tooltip00005" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00005"><span class="tt kwbu">mortal</span> <span class="tt kwbu">wounds</span></span> denoted by ‘x’ (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6").<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> A <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model with the Deadly Demise D3 ability is destroyed. Before any models disembark and before removing it from play, its controlling player rolls one D6, getting a 6. There are three units within 6" of that destroyed model, so its controlling player rolls one D3 for each of them, inflicting mortal wounds accordingly.</div><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Deadly Demise x:</b> When this model is destroyed, roll one D6. On a 6, each unit within 6" suffers ‘x’ mortal wounds.</li></ul></div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00007"><p>The <span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHA"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span> keyword is used in the following Leagues of Votann datasheets:</p><span class="contents"><div LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Hekaton-Land-Fortress" class="contentColor">Hekaton Land Fortress</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Ironkin-Steeljacks-with-Heavy-Volkanite-Disintegrators" class="contentColor">Ironkin Steeljacks with Heavy Volkanite Disintegrators</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Ironkin-Steeljacks-with-Melee-Weapons" class="contentColor">Ironkin Steeljacks with Melee Weapons</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Kapricus-Defenders" class="contentColor">Kapricus Defenders</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div></div></span></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00008"><div class="tooltip_header">Prioritised Efficiency</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/#Prioritised-Efficiency"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div><p class="ShowFluff legend2">As the Kin truth describes, Luck has. Need keeps. Toil earns. The kinhost’s ability to efficiently switch its strategic footing embodies this wisdom. It results from swift assessments of a foe’s possessions, how best to seize them for the kindred’s need and then to stubbornly toil to hold them against counterattacks.</p><div class="Columns2">If your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#3.-Select-Army-Faction">Army Faction</a> is <span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHA"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span>, <span class="tooltip00007" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00007" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00007"><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHA"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span></span> units from your army have one of two abilities: Hostile Acquisition or Fortify Takeover (see below). During the battle, which ability those <span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHA"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span> units have will change depending on how many Yield points (YP) you currently have:<br><ul><li>At the start of the battle, your units have Hostile Acquisition until the start of your next <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#COMMAND-PHASE">Command phase</a>.</li><li>At the end of your Command phase, if you have fewer than 7YP, your units have Hostile Acquisition until the start of your next Command phase.</li><li>At the end of your Command phase, if you have 7YP or more, your units have Fortify Takeover until the start of your next Command phase.</li></ul><br><span class="hi_custom">Hostile Acquisition</span><br><ul><li>Each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit within range of one or more <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Objective-Markers">objective markers</a>, add 1 to the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#1.-Hit-Roll">Hit roll</a>.</li><li>You can re-roll <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Advance-Moves">Advance</a> and <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Charging-with-a-Unit">Charge rolls</a> made for this unit.</li></ul><br><span class="hi_custom">Fortify Takeover</span><br><ul><li>Each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit, if this unit is within range of one or more objective markers you control, add 1 to the Hit roll.</li><li>Each time an attack targets this unit, if the Strength characteristic of that attack is greater than this unit’s Toughness characteristic and this unit is not a <span class="kwb">VEHICLE</span>, subtract 1 from the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Wound-Roll">Wound roll</a>.</li></ul><br><span class="hi_custom">Yield Points</span><br>At the end of each player’s Command phase, you gain 1YP if you control one or more objective markers within your deployment zone, and, from the second <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#The-Battle-Round">battle round</a> onwards, you gain 1 additional YP for each of the following conditions you satisfy:<br><ul><li>You control one or more objective markers not within your deployment zone.</li><li>You control two or more objective markers not within your deployment zone.</li><li>You control more objective markers than your opponent.</li></ul><br>In your turn, YP gained in this way are always gained before checking which ability <span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHA"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span> units from your army have.<br><br>Whenever a rule allows you to spend YP, reduce the number of YP you have by that amount (your YP cannot go below 0), otherwise you cannot use that rule.<br><br><b>Designer’s Note:</b> <i>This means you can use rules that let you gain or spend YP to influence which ability your units have - Hostile Acquisition or Fortify Takeover - depending on which will be most beneficial to you. Remember that your army only changes ability at the end of your Command phase, so you will - sometimes need to plan ahead if you want your army to stay in or change ability.</i><br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> In her third turn, Freida’s units had the Hostile Acquisition ability, as she had 6YP at the end of her Command phase. In her fourth turn, at the end of her Command phase, she controls one objective marker within her deployment zone, and one objective marker not within her deployment zone, and her opponent controls two objective markers. Freida gains 2YP, so she now has 8YP, and her units now have the Fortify Takeover ability instead.<br><br>During her turn, Freida uses a Stratagem that allows her to spend YP. She spends 2YP, bringing her total down to 6YP. However, units from her army still have Fortify Takeover - it will only change if, at the end of her next Command phase, she still has fewer than 7YP.</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00009"><p>The <span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHA"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span> and <span class="kwb">INFANTRY</span> keywords are used in the following Leagues of Votann datasheets:</p><span class="contents"><div style="clear:both; style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO LVVS" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Cthonian-Earthshakers" class="contentColor">Cthonian Earthshakers</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Einhyr-Hearthguard" class="contentColor">Einhyr Hearthguard</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO LVVS" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Hernkyn-Yaegirs" class="contentColor">Hernkyn Yaegirs</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Ironkin-Steeljacks-with-Heavy-Volkanite-Disintegrators" class="contentColor">Ironkin Steeljacks with Heavy Volkanite Disintegrators</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Ironkin-Steeljacks-with-Melee-Weapons" class="contentColor">Ironkin Steeljacks with Melee Weapons</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div></span></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00010"><div class="Corner33 abWrap BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="abFlex"><div class="abDiamondWrap"><div class="abDiamond"><div class="abIcon"></div></div></div><div class="abLine"></div><div class="abNameWrap"><div class="abName">LEADER</div></div></div><p style="padding-bottom:8px" class="ShowFluff stratLegend2">Mighty heroes fight at the forefront of battle.</p>Some <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> units have ‘Leader’ listed on their datasheets. Such <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> units are known as Leaders, and the units they can lead – known as their Bodyguard units – are listed on their datasheet.<br><br>During the Declare Battle Formations step, for each Leader in your army, if your army also includes one or more of that Leader’s Bodyguard units, you can select one of those Bodyguard units. That Leader will then attach to that Bodyguard unit for the duration of the battle and is said to be leading that unit. Each Bodyguard unit can only have one Leader attached to it.<br><br>While a Bodyguard unit contains a Leader, it is known as an Attached unit and, with the exception of rules that are triggered when units are <span class="tooltip00001" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00001"><span class="tt kwbu">destroyed</span></span>, it is treated as a single unit for all rules purposes. Each time an attack targets an Attached unit, until the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks, you must use the Toughness characteristic of the Bodyguard models in that unit, even if a Leader in that unit has a different Toughness characteristic. Each time an attack sucessfully wounds an Attached unit, that attack cannot be allocated to a <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model in that unit, even if that <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model has lost one or more wounds or has already had attacks allocated to it this phase. As soon as the last Bodyguard model in an Attached unit has been destroyed, any attacks made against that unit that have yet to be allocated can then be allocated to <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> models in that unit.<br><br>Each time the last model in a Bodyguard unit is destroyed, each <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> unit that is part of that Attached unit is no longer part of an Attached unit. It becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks.<br><br>Each time the last model in a <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> unit that is attached to a Bodyguard unit is destroyed and there is not another <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> unit attached, that Attached unit’s Bodyguard unit is no longer part of an Attached unit. It becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. <br><br>Each time a unit that is part of an Attached unit is destroyed, it does not have the keywords of any other units that make up that Attached unit (unless it has those keywords on its own datasheet) for the purposes of any rules that would be triggered when that unit is destroyed.<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> <i>If you only destroy the Bodyguard unit that is part of an Attached unit, you have not destroyed a <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> unit. If you only destroy the <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> unit that is part of an Attached unit, or if you destroy the whole Attached unit, you have destroyed one <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> unit.</i></div><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>Before the battle, <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> units with the Leader ability can be attached to one of their Bodyguard units to form an Attached unit.</li><li>Attached units can only contain one Leader.</li><li>Attacks cannot be allocated to <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> models in Attached units.</li></ul></div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00011"><div class="Corner33 abWrap BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="abFlex"><div class="abDiamondWrap"><div class="abDiamond"><div class="abIcon"></div></div></div><div class="abLine"></div><div class="abNameWrap"><div class="abName">LONE OPERATIVE</div></div></div><p style="padding-bottom:8px" class="ShowFluff stratLegend2">Assassins and other covert agents are difficult to track and pinpoint in the swirling maelstrom of battle.</p>Unless part of an Attached unit (see <span class="tooltip00010" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00010"><span class="tt kwbu">Leader</span></span>), this unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 12".</div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00012"><div class="tooltip_header">Sustained Hits</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Sustained-Hits"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Some weapons strike in a flurry of blows, tearing the foe apart with relentless ferocity.</p>Weapons with <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[SUSTAINED HITS X]</span> in their profile are known as Sustained Hits weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#1.-Hit-Roll">Critical Hit</a> is rolled, that attack scores a number of additional hits on the target as denoted by ‘x’.<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> A model makes an attack with a melee weapon with the <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[SUSTAINED HITS 2]</span> ability. If the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#1.-Hit-Roll">Hit roll</a> is an unmodified 6 (a Critical Hit), then that attack scores a total of 3 hits on the target (1 from the successful Hit roll of 6, and 2 from the <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[SUSTAINED HITS 2]</span> ability).</div><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><span class="kwb2 bluefont">[SUSTAINED HITS X]</span>: Each Critical Hit scores ‘x’ additional hits on the target.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_contenttooltipPointy"><i>Before selecting targets for this weapon, select one of its profiles to make attacks with.</i></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00014"><div class="tooltip_header">Rapid Fire</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Rapid-Fire"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Rapid fire weapons are capable of long-ranged precision shots or controlled bursts at nearby targets.</p>Weapons with <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[RAPID FIRE X]</span> in their profile are known as Rapid Fire weapons. Each time such a weapon targets a unit within half that weapon’s range, the Attacks characteristic of that weapon is increased by the amount denoted by ‘x’.<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> A model targets a unit that is within half range of a weapon with an Attacks characteristic of 1 and the <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[RAPID FIRE 1]</span> ability. That weapon therefore makes two attacks at the target, and you make two <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#1.-Hit-Roll">Hit rolls</a>.</div><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><span class="kwb2 bluefont">[RAPID FIRE X]</span>: Increase the Attacks by ‘x’ when targeting units within half range.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_contentLeaguesofVotann"><p>The <span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span class="kwb">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2">OF</span> <span
