href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Cthonian-Beserks" class="contentColor">Cthonian Beserks</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Cthonian-Earthshakers" class="contentColor">Cthonian Earthshakers</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Einhyr-Hearthguard" class="contentColor">Einhyr Hearthguard</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO LVVS" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Hernkyn-Yaegirs" class="contentColor">Hernkyn Yaegirs</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Ironkin-Steeljacks-with-Heavy-Volkanite-Disintegrators" class="contentColor">Ironkin Steeljacks with Heavy Volkanite Disintegrators</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Ironkin-Steeljacks-with-Melee-Weapons" class="contentColor">Ironkin Steeljacks with Melee Weapons</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div></span></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_contentCharacter"><p>The <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> keyword is used in the following Leagues of Votann datasheets:</p><span class="contents"><div style="clear:both; margin-bottom:8px;"><div class="BatRole">Characters</div><div class="BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Arkanyst-Evaluator" class="contentColor">Arkanyst Evaluator</a></div></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO LVVS" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Brôkhyr-Iron-master" class="contentColor">Brôkhyr Iron-master</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Buri-Aegnirssen" class="contentColor">Buri Aegnirssen</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Einhyr-Champion" class="contentColor">Einhyr Champion</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO LVVS" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Grimnyr" class="contentColor">Grimnyr</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO LVVS" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#K-hl" class="contentColor">Kâhl</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Memnyr-Strategist" class="contentColor">Memnyr Strategist</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO LVVS" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Ûthar-the-Destined" class="contentColor">Ûthar the Destined</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div></span></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00018"><div class="tooltip_header">Attached Units</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Attached-Units"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
Some <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> units have the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Normal-Moves">Leader</a> ability, which lets them merge with other units (known as Bodyguard units) to form an Attached unit.<br><br>The <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Starting-Strength">Starting Strength</a> of an Attached unit is equal to the combined Starting Strengths of all of its units (i.e. the number of models in the Leader unit added to the number of models in the Bodyguard unit). If either the Leader unit or the Bodyguard unit in an Attached unit is <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Destroyed">destroyed</a>, the Starting Strength of the remaining unit is changed to be equal to its original Starting Strength.<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> A Primaris Captain (Starting Strength 1) is attached to a unit of Intercessors (Starting Strength 5). This Attached unit has a Starting Strength of 6. If all the Intercessors are destroyed, the remaining Primaris Captain would revert to having a Starting Strength of 1.</div><br>For the purposes of rules that are triggered when a unit is destroyed, such rules are still triggered when one of the individual units that made up an Attached unit is destroyed (the Leader or the Bodyguard unit).<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> If a rule awards you with 1VP each time an enemy unit is destroyed, and you target an Attached unit, you would gain 1VP if the Bodyguard unit is destroyed and 1VP if the Leader unit is destroyed (for a total of 2VP).</div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00019"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEither"><div class="str10Name">BATTLEFIELD COMMAND</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(40%) sepia(12%) saturate(2210%) hue-rotate(121deg) brightness(94%) contrast(88%);" ;="" class="str10Any str10ColorEither"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Boarding Actions – Strategic Ploy Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">The commander's authoritative tone cuts through the din of battle, guiding the actions of nearby troops.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Start or end of any phase.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One <span class="tooltip00010" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00010"><span class="tt kwbu">Leader</span></span> unit from your army and one friendly <span class="tooltip00018" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00018"><span class="tt kwbu">Bodyguard</span></span> unit within 6" of it that it could normally be attached to.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Select one of your Leader unit’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/boarding-action-rules/#Leader-Abilities">Leader abilities</a>. Until the start of your next <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#COMMAND-PHASE">Command phase</a>, that Bodyguard unit is treated as being led by that Leader unit for the purposes of that Leader ability.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>RESTRICTIONS:</b></span> Once you target a unit with this Stratagem, that unit cannot be targeted with it again until your next Command phase.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00020"><div class="tooltip_header">Hit Roll</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#1.-Hit-Roll"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
When a model makes an attack, make one Hit roll for that attack by rolling one D6. If the result of the Hit roll is greater than or equal to the attack’s Ballistic Skill (BS) characteristic (if the attack is being made with a ranged weapon) or its Weapon Skill (WS) characteristic (if the attack is being made with a melee weapon), then that Hit roll is successful and scores one hit against the target unit. Otherwise, the attack fails and the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Making-Attacks">attack sequence</a> ends.<br><br>An unmodified Hit roll of 6 is called a Critical Hit and is always successful. An unmodified Hit roll of 1 always fails. A Hit roll can never be modified by more than -1 or +1.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Hit Roll (Ranged Attack):</b> A hit is scored if the D6 result equals or exceeds that attack’s BS.</li><li><b>Hit Roll (Melee Attack):</b> A hit is scored if the D6 result equals or exceeds that attack’s WS.</li><li><b>Critical Hit:</b> Unmodified Hit roll of 6. Always successful.</li><li>An unmodified Hit roll of 1 always fails.</li><li>A Hit roll can never be modified by more than -1 or +1.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00021"><div class="tooltip_header">Wound Roll</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Wound-Roll"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
Each time an attack scores a hit against a target unit, make a Wound roll for that attack by rolling one D6 to see if that attack successfully wounds the target unit. The result required is determined by comparing the attack’s Strength (S) characteristic with the target’s Toughness (T) characteristic, as shown below.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid" style="" "=""><table width="0" border="0" bordercolor="#888" cellpadding="4" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse:collapse;border-width:0px"><tbody><tr><td><table class=" customTable tablefont" border="1" bordercolor="#888" cellpadding="4" style="border-collapse:collapse;border-color:#888888;border-width:0px"><tbody><tr><td colspan="2" style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none solid none;background-color:rgb(57,82,91);color:rgb(254,254,254);vertical-align:bottom;font-size:26px;width:0px;height:0px;"><b>WOUND ROLL</b></td></tr><tr><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none solid none;background-color:rgb(120,137,145);color:rgb(254,254,254);text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;font-size:22px;width:360px;height:0px;"><b>ATTACK’S STRENGTH VS TARGET’S TOUGHNESS</b></td><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none solid none;background-color:rgb(120,137,145);color:rgb(254,254,254);text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;font-size:22px;width:120px;height:0px;"><b>D6 RESULT REQUIRED</b></td></tr><tr><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:left;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:360px;height:0px;">Strength is <span class="redfont">TWICE (or more than twice)</span> the Toughness.</td><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:120px;height:0px;"><b><div style="display:flex; justify-content:center"><img src="/wh40k10ed/img/d2.png"><div style="margin:auto 10px auto 10px">+</div></div></b></td></tr><tr><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:left;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:360px;height:0px;">Strength is <span class="redfont">GREATER</span> than the Toughness.</td><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:120px;height:0px;"><b><div style="display:flex; justify-content:center"><img src="/wh40k10ed/img/d3.png"><div style="margin:auto 10px auto 10px">+</div></div></b></td></tr><tr><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:left;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:360px;height:0px;">Strength is <span class="redfont">EQUAL</span> to the Toughness.</td><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:120px;height:0px;"><b><div style="display:flex; justify-content:center"><img src="/wh40k10ed/img/d4.png"><div style="margin:auto 10px auto 10px">+</div></div></b></td></tr><tr><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:left;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:360px;height:0px;">Strength is <span class="redfont">LESS</span> than the Toughness.</td><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:120px;height:0px;"><b><div style="display:flex; justify-content:center"><img src="/wh40k10ed/img/d5.png"><div style="margin:auto 10px auto 10px">+</div></div></b></td></tr><tr><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:left;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:360px;height:0px;">Strength is <span class="redfont">HALF (or less than half)</span> the Toughness.</td><td style="border-width:1px 1px 1px 1px;border-style:none none dotted none;text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;font-size:18px;width:120px;height:0px;"><b><div style="display:flex; justify-content:center"><img src="/wh40k10ed/img/d6.png"><div style="margin:auto 10px auto 10px">+</div></div></b></td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr></tbody></table></div><br>If the result of the Wound roll is greater than or equal to the required number shown in the table above, then that Wound roll is successful and scores one wound against the target unit. Otherwise, the attack fails and the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Making-Attacks">attack sequence</a> ends.<br><br>An unmodified Wound roll of 6 is called a Critical Wound and is always successful. An unmodified Wound roll of 1 always fails. A Wound roll can never be modified by more than -1 or +1.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Critical Wound:</b> Unmodified Wound roll of 6. Always successful.</li><li>An unmodified Wound roll of 1 always fails.</li><li>A Wound roll can never be modified by more than -1 or +1.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00022"><div class="tooltip_header">Saving Throw</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#4.-Saving-Throw"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
The player controlling the target unit then makes one saving throw. By default, this will be an armour saving throw using their model’s Save (Sv) characteristic, but some models have <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Invulnerable-Saves">invulnerable saves</a> that can be used instead (see below). To make an armour saving throw, roll one D6, then modify the result by the Armour Penetration (AP) characteristic of the attack. For example, if the attack has an AP of -1, then 1 is subtracted from the saving throw.<br><br>If the result is greater than or equal to the Save characteristic of the model the attack was allocated to, then that saving throw is successful and the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Making-Attacks">attack sequence</a> ends. Otherwise, that saving throw fails and that model <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#5.-Inflict-Damage">suffers damage</a>.<br><br>An unmodified saving throw of 1 always fails. A saving throw can never be improved by more than +1.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Saving Throw:</b> Roll one D6 and modify by the attack’s AP. If the result is less than the Save of the model being rolled for, the saving throw is failed and that model suffers damage. Otherwise, that attack is saved.</li><li>An unmodified saving throw of 1 always fails.</li><li>A saving throw can never be improved by more than +1.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00023"><div class="tooltip_header">Advance Moves</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Advance-Moves"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
When a unit Advances, make an Advance roll for that unit by rolling one D6. Add the result in inches to the Move characteristic of each model in that unit until the end of the phase. Each model in that unit can then make an Advance move by moving a distance in inches less than or equal to this total, but no model can be moved within <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range">Engagement Range</a> of enemy models. A unit cannot shoot or <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#CHARGE-PHASE">declare a charge</a> in the same turn that it Advanced.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Advance Move:</b> Models move up to M+D6".</li><li>Cannot move within Engagement Range of any enemy models.</li><li>Units that Advance cannot shoot or charge this turn.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00024"><div class="tooltip_header">Charging with a Unit</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Charging-with-a-Unit"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
Once you have selected an eligible unit to declare a charge, you must select one or more enemy units within 12" of it as the targets of that charge. The targets of a charge do not need to be visible to the charging unit.<br><br>You then make a Charge roll for the charging unit by rolling 2D6. The result is the maximum number of inches each model in that unit can be moved if a Charge move is possible. For a Charge move to be possible, the Charge roll must be sufficient to enable the charging unit to end that move:<br><ul><li>Within <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range">Engagement Range</a> of every unit that you selected as a target of the charge.</li><li>Without moving within Engagement Range of any enemy units that were not a target of the charge.</li><li>In <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Unit-Coherency">Unit Coherency</a>.</li></ul>If any of these conditions cannot be met, the charge fails and no models in the charging unit move this phase. Otherwise, the charge is successful and the models in the charging unit make a Charge move – move each model a distance in inches up to the result of the Charge roll. When doing so, each model in the charging unit must end its Charge move closer to one of the units selected as a target of its charge. If you can also move a charging model so that it ends its Charge move in base-to-base contact with one or more enemy models while still enabling the charging unit to end its move satisfying all of the conditions above, you must do so. The controlling player chooses the order in which to move their models.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Charge Roll:</b> 2D6".</li><li>Targets of a charge must be within 12" but do not need to be visible.</li><li>If the distance rolled is insufficient to move within Engagement Range of all targets while maintaining Unit Coherency, the charge fails.</li><li>Cannot move within Engagement Range of any unit that was not a target of the charge.</li><li>If the charge is successful, each model makes a Charge move less than or equal to the Charge roll, and must move into base-to-base contact with an enemy model if possible.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00025"><div class="tooltip_header">Fall Back Moves</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Fall-Back-Moves"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<div class="BreakInsideAvoid">When a unit Falls Back, each model in that unit can make a Fall Back move by moving a distance in inches less than or equal to its Move characteristic, and when doing so you can move it within <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range">Engagement Range</a> of enemy models, provided it does not end that move within Engagement Range of any enemy models – if this is not possible, that unit cannot Fall Back.<br><br>A unit cannot shoot or declare a charge in the same turn that it Fell Back.<br><br><span class="hi_custom">Desperate Escape Tests</span><br>Unlike when making other types of move, models can move over enemy models when making a Fall Back move as if those enemy models were not there, but you must take a Desperate Escape test for each model that will do so (excluding models that are <span class="kwb">TITANIC</span> or can <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Flying"><span class="kwb">FLY</span></a>) before any models in that unit are moved. In addition, if a unit is <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Battle-shock">Battle-shocked</a> when it is selected to Fall Back, you must take a Desperate Escape test for every model in that unit before any are moved.<br><br></div>Each time you take a Desperate Escape test for a model, roll one D6. For each roll of 1-2, one model from the unit that is Falling Back is <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Destroyed">destroyed</a> (selected by you). The same model can only ever trigger one Desperate Escape test per phase.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Fall Back Move:</b> Models move up to M".</li><li>Units that Fall Back cannot shoot or declare a charge in the same turn.</li><li>Models can move over enemy models when Falling Back, but you must take Desperate Escape tests for them before they do so (excluding models that are <span class="kwb">TITANIC</span> or can <span class="kwb">FLY</span>).</li><li>If a Battle-shocked unit is selected to Fall Back, take a Desperate Escape test for every model in that unit.</li><li><b>Desperate Escape Test:</b> Roll one D6. On a 1-2, one model from that unit is destroyed.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00026"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEither"><div class="str10Name">COMMAND RE-ROLL</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(40%) sepia(12%) saturate(2210%) hue-rotate(121deg) brightness(94%) contrast(88%);" ;="" class="str10Any str10ColorEither"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Boarding Actions – Epic Deed Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">A great commander can bend even the vagaries of fate and fortune to their will, the better to ensure victory.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Any phase, just after you have made a <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a <span class="tooltip00021" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00021"><span class="tt kwbu">Wound</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a Damage roll, a <span class="tooltip00022" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00022"><span class="tt kwbu">saving</span> <span class="tt kwbu">throw</span></span>, an <span class="tooltip00023" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00023"><span class="tt kwbu">Advance</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a <span class="tooltip00024" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00024"><span class="tt kwbu">Charge</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a <span class="tooltip00025" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00025"><span class="tt kwbu">Desperate</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Escape</span> <span class="tt kwbu">test</span></span>, a <span class="tooltip00003" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00003"><span class="tt kwbu">Hazardous</span> <span class="tt kwbu">test</span></span>, or just after you have rolled the dice to determine the number of attacks made with a weapon, for an attack, model or unit from your army.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>TARGET:</b></span> That unit or model from your army.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> You re-roll that roll, test or saving throw.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00027"><div class="tooltip_header">2. Battle-shock</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Battle-shock"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
In this step, you must take a Battle-shock test for each of your units on the battlefield that is <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Below-Half-strength">Below Half-strength</a>. To do so, roll 2D6: if the result is greater than or equal to the best Leadership characteristic in that unit, the test is passed; otherwise, the test is failed and, until the start of your next Command phase, that unit is Battle-shocked.<br><br>While a unit is Battle-shocked:<br><ul><li>The <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Profiles">Objective Control</a> characteristic of all of its models is 0.</li><li>If it Falls Back, you must take a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Fall-Back-Moves">Desperate Escape test</a> for every model in that unit.</li><li>Its controlling player cannot use <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Stratagems">Stratagems</a> to affect that unit.</li></ul><br>Once you have taken Battle-shock tests for all of your units that require them, your Command phase ends and you progress to your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#MOVEMENT-PHASE">Movement phase</a>.<br><br>In this step, if for any reason a unit is forced to take a Battle-shock test for being below its Starting Strength, unless otherwise stated, that unit does not also have to take a Battle-shock test for being Below Half-strength. While a unit is Battle-shocked, all models in that unit are also Battle-shocked.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>Take a Battle-shock test for each unit from your army on the battlefield that is Below Half-strength.</li><li>Roll 2D6: if the result is greater than or equal to the unit’s Leadership, the test is passed. Otherwise, the unit is Battle-shocked until the start of your next Command phase.</li><li>Battle-shocked units have an OC of 0 and their controlling player cannot use Stratagems to affect them.</li><li>Battle-shocked units must take Desperate Escape tests if they Fall Back.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00028"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorYour"><div class="str10Name">INSANE BRAVERY</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(23%) sepia(88%) saturate(2002%) hue-rotate(179deg) brightness(87%) contrast(93%);" ;="" class="str10Command str10ColorYour"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Boarding Actions – Epic Deed Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">Indifferent to their own survival, these warriors hold their ground against seemingly impossible odds.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>WHEN:</b></span> <span class="tooltip00027" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00027"><span class="tt kwbu">Battle-shock</span></span> step of your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#COMMAND-PHASE">Command phase</a>, just after you have failed a <span class="tooltip00027" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00027"><span class="tt kwbu">Battle-shock</span> <span class="tt kwbu">test</span></span> taken for a unit from your army.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>TARGET:</b></span> The unit from your army that Battle-shock test was just taken for (even though your Battle-shocked units cannot normally be affected by your Stratagems).<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Your unit is treated as having passed that test instead, and is not Battle-shocked as a result.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00029"><div class="tooltip_header">Blast</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Blast"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">High-explosives can fell several warriors in a single blast, but firing them where your comrades will get caught in the ensuing detonation is simply unwise.</p>Weapons with <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[BLAST]</span> in their profile are known as Blast weapons, and they make a random number of attacks. Each time you determine how many attacks are made with a Blast weapon, add 1 to the result for every five models that were in the target unit when you selected it as the target (rounding down). Blast weapons can never be used to make attacks against a unit that is within Engagement Range of one or more units from the attacking model’s army (including its own unit).<br><br><div class="redExample"><b>Example:</b> If a weapon with the <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[BLAST]</span> ability and an Attacks characteristic of 2D6 targets a unit that contains 11 models, and the roll to determine how many attacks are made is a 9, a total of 11 attacks would be made against that unit.</div><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>Add 1 to the Attacks characteristic for every five models in the target unit (rounding down).</li><li>Can never be used against a target that is within Engagement Range of any units from the attacking model’s army (including its own).</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00030"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorYour"><div class="str10Name">EXPLOSIVE CLEARANCE</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(23%) sepia(88%) saturate(2002%) hue-rotate(179deg) brightness(87%) contrast(93%);" ;="" class="str10Shooting str10ColorYour"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Boarding Actions – Battle Tactic Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">A well-placed explosive munition in confined shipboard spaces can cause bloody carnage.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Select one model in your unit that is equipped with a weapon with the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><span class="tooltip00029" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00029"><span class="tt kwbu">[BLAST]</span></span></span> ability. Until the end of the phase, when determining how many models are in the target unit for the purpose of that ability, include models that are not <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/boarding-action-rules/#Visibility">visible</a> to the attacking model. In addition, attacks made with that weapon can be allocated to models that are not visible to the attacking model.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00031"><div class="tooltip_header">Engagement Range</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
Engagement Range represents the zone of threat that models present to their enemies. While a model is within 1" horizontally and 5" vertically of an enemy model, those models – and their units – are within Engagement Range of each other.<br><br>Models cannot be set up or end a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Normal-Moves">Normal</a>, <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Advance-Moves">Advance</a> or <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Fall-Back-Moves">Fall Back move</a> within Engagement Range of any enemy models. If for any reason a model cannot meet this condition, that model is <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Destroyed">destroyed</a>.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Engagement Range:</b> Within 1" horizontally and 5" vertically.</li><li>Models cannot be set up or end a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move within Engagement Range of any enemy models.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00032"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEither"><div class="str10Name">COUNTER-OFFENSIVE</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(40%) sepia(12%) saturate(2210%) hue-rotate(121deg) brightness(94%) contrast(88%);" ;="" class="str10Fight str10ColorEither"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">2CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Boarding Actions – Strategic Ploy Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">In close-quarters combat, the slightest hesitation can leave an opening for a swift foe to exploit.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>WHEN:</b></span> <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#FIGHT-PHASE">Fight phase</a>, just after an enemy unit has fought.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One unit from your army that is within <span class="tooltip00031" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00031"><span class="tt kwbu">Engagement</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Range</span></span> of one or more enemy units and that has not already been selected to fight this phase.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Your unit fights next.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00033"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorYour"><div class="str10Name">INEXORABLE EFFICIENCY</div>

class="str10ColorYour"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One <span class="tooltip00007" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00007" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00007"><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span kwbu">VOTANN</span></span></span> unit from your army.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Until the end of the phase, your unit is eligible to shoot in a turn in which it <span class="tooltip00025" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00025"><span class="tt kwbu">Fell</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Back</span></span>.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00034"><p>The <span class="kwb">KAPRICUS</span> keyword is used in the following Leagues of Votann datasheets:</p><span class="contents"><div style="clear:both; margin-bottom:8px;"><div class="BatRole">Dedicated Transports</div><div class="BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Kapricus-Carrier" class="contentColor">Kapricus Carrier</a></div></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div><div style="clear:both; margin-bottom:8px;"><div class="BatRole">Other</div><div class="BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Kapricus-Defenders" class="contentColor">Kapricus Defenders</a></div></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div></span></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00035"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEnemy"><div class="str10Name">VENGEANCE FLARE</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(10%) sepia(76%) saturate(6071%) hue-rotate(352deg) brightness(102%) contrast(99%);" ;="" class="str10Shooting str10ColorEnemy"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">2CP</div></div></div></div>

<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Your opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, just after an enemy unit has shot.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One <span class="tooltip00009" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00009" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00009"><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span> <span class="kwb kwbu">INFANTRY</span></span> unit that was hit by one or more of those attacks.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Each time you use this Stratagem, you can spend 2YP. Select one friendly <span class="tooltip00034" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00034" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00034"><span class="kwb kwbu">KAPRICUS</span></span> or <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Sagitaur"><span class="kwb kwbo">SAGITAUR</span></a> unit within 6" of your <span class="kwb">INFANTRY</span> unit. If you spent YP during this use of this Stratagem, you can select one friendly <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Hekaton-Land-Fortress"><span class="kwb kwbo">HEKATON</span> <span class="kwb kwbo">LAND</span> <span class="kwb kwbo">FORTRESS</span></a> unit within 6" of your <span class="kwb">INFANTRY</span> unit instead. The selected unit can shoot as if it were your Shooting phase. When doing so, models in the selected unit can only target that enemy unit (and only if it is an eligible target).</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00036"><div class="tooltip_header">Re-rolls</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Re-rolls"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
Some rules allow you to re-roll a dice roll, which means you get to roll some or all of the dice again. If a rule allows you to re-roll a dice roll that was made by adding several dice together (e.g. 2D6, 3D6, etc.) then, unless otherwise stated, you must re-roll all of those dice again.<br><br>You can never re-roll a dice more than once, and re-rolls happen before modifiers (if any) are applied. Rules that refer to the value of an ‘unmodified’ dice roll are referring to the dice result after any re-rolls, but before any modifiers are applied.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Unmodified Dice:</b> the result after re-rolls, but before any modifiers.</li><li>A dice can never be re-rolled more than once.</li><li>You must re-roll all dice if several need adding together (e.g. 2D6).</li><li>Re-rolls are applied before any modifiers.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00037"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEither"><div class="str10Name">COMMAND RE-ROLL</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(40%) sepia(12%) saturate(2210%) hue-rotate(121deg) brightness(94%) contrast(88%);" ;="" class="str10Any str10ColorEither"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Core – Battle Tactic Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">A great commander can bend even the vagaries of fate and fortune to their will, the better to ensure victory.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Any phase, just after you make an <span class="tooltip00023" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00023"><span class="tt kwbu">Advance</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a <span class="tooltip00024" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00024"><span class="tt kwbu">Charge</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a <span class="tooltip00025" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00025"><span class="tt kwbu">Desperate</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Escape</span> <span class="tt kwbu">test</span></span> or a <span class="tooltip00003" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00003"><span class="tt kwbu">Hazardous</span> <span class="tt kwbu">test</span></span> for a unit from your army, or a <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a <span class="tooltip00021" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00021"><span class="tt kwbu">Wound</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>, a Damage roll or a <span class="tooltip00022" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00022"><span class="tt kwbu">saving</span> <span class="tt kwbu">throw</span></span> for a model in that unit, or a roll to determine the number of attacks made with a weapon equipped by a model in that unit. If you are using <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Fast-Dice-Rolling">fast dice rolling</a>, this Stratagem can still be used after rolling multiple rolls or saving throws at once.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>TARGET:</b></span> That unit or model from your army.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> You <span class="tooltip00036" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00036"><span class="tt kwbu">re-roll</span></span> that roll, test or saving throw. If you are using fast dice rolling, select one of those rolls or saving throws to re-roll.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00038"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorYour"><div class="str10Name">INSANE BRAVERY</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(23%) sepia(88%) saturate(2002%) hue-rotate(179deg) brightness(87%) contrast(93%);" ;="" class="str10Command str10ColorYour"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Core – Epic Deed Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">Indifferent to their own survival, these warriors hold their ground against seemingly impossible odds.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>WHEN:</b></span> <span class="tooltip00027" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00027"><span class="tt kwbu">Battle-shock</span></span> step of your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#COMMAND-PHASE">Command phase</a>, just before you take a <span class="tooltip00027" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00027"><span class="tt kwbu">Battle-shock</span> <span class="tt kwbu">test</span></span> for a unit from your army.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>TARGET:</b></span> That unit from your army.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Your unit automatically passes that Battle-shock test.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>RESTRICTIONS:</b></span> You cannot use this Stratagem more than once per battle.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00039"><div class="tooltip_header">Normal Moves</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Normal-Moves"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
When a unit makes a Normal move, each model in that unit can move a distance in inches less than or equal to its Move (M) characteristic, but no model can be moved within <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range">Engagement Range</a> of any enemy models. A unit cannot make more than one Normal move per phase.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Normal Move:</b> Models move up to M".</li><li>Cannot move within Engagement Range of any enemy models.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00040"><div class="tooltip_header">Unit Visible</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Unit-Visible"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
If one or more models in a unit is visible to the observing model, then that model’s unit is visible to the observing model.<br><br><div class=" img-opa" style="clear:both;display:flex;justify-content:center;align-items:center;"><img border="0" src="/wh40k10ed/img/DeterminingVisibility2.png" style="margin:0px"></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00041"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEnemy"><div class="str10Name">FIRE OVERWATCH</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(10%) sepia(76%) saturate(6071%) hue-rotate(352deg) brightness(102%) contrast(99%);" ;="" class="str10Movement str10ColorEnemy"></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(10%) sepia(76%) saturate(6071%) hue-rotate(352deg) brightness(102%) contrast(99%);" class="str10Charge str10ColorEnemy"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Core – Strategic Ploy Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">A hail of wildfire can drive back advancing foes.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Your opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#MOVEMENT-PHASE">Movement</a> or <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#CHARGE-PHASE">Charge phase</a>, just after an enemy unit is set up or when an enemy unit starts or ends a <span class="tooltip00039" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00039"><span class="tt kwbu">Normal</span></span>, <span class="tooltip00023" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00023"><span class="tt kwbu">Advance</span></span> or <span class="tooltip00025" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00025"><span class="tt kwbu">Fall</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Back</span> <span class="tt kwbu">move</span></span>, or <span class="tooltip00024" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00024"><span class="tt kwbu">declares</span> <span class="tt kwbu">a</span> <span class="tt kwbu">charge</span></span>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One unit from your army that is within 24" of that enemy unit and that would be eligible to shoot if it were your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> If that enemy <span class="tooltip00040" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00040"><span class="tt kwbu">unit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">is</span> <span class="tt kwbu">visible</span></span> to your unit, your unit can shoot that enemy unit as if it were your Shooting phase.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>RESTRICTIONS:</b></span> You cannot target a <span class="kwb">TITANIC</span> unit with this Stratagem. Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes a ranged attack, an unmodified <span class="tooltip00020" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00020"><span class="tt kwbu">Hit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span> of 6 is required to score a hit, irrespective of the attacking weapon’s Ballistic Skill or any modifiers. You can only use this Stratagem once per turn.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00042"><div class="Corner33 abWrap BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="abFlex"><div class="abDiamondWrap"><div class="abDiamond"><div class="abIcon"></div></div></div><div class="abLine"></div><div class="abNameWrap"><div class="abName">DEEP STRIKE</div></div></div><p style="padding-bottom:8px" class="ShowFluff stratLegend2">Some units make their way to battle via tunnelling, teleportation, high-altitude descent or other extraordinary means that allow them to appear suddenly in the thick of the fighting.</p>During the Declare Battle Formations step, if every model in a unit has this ability, you can set it up in Reserves instead of setting it up on the battlefield. If you do, in the Reinforcements step of one of your Movement phases you can set up this unit anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models. If a unit with the Deep Strike ability arrives from Strategic Reserves, the controlling player can choose for that unit to be set up either using the rules for Strategic Reserves or using the Deep Strike ability. <br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>Unit can be set up in Reserves instead of on the battlefield.</li><li>Unit can be set up in your Reinforcements step, more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models.</li></ul></div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00043"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEnemy"><div class="str10Name">RAPID INGRESS</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(10%) sepia(76%) saturate(6071%) hue-rotate(352deg) brightness(102%) contrast(99%);" ;="" class="str10Movement str10ColorEnemy"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Core – Strategic Ploy Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">Be it cunning strategy, potent technology or supernatural ritual, there are many means by which a commander may hasten their warriors’ onset.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>WHEN:</b></span> End of your opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#MOVEMENT-PHASE">Movement phase</a>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One unit from your army that is in <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Reinforcements">Reserves</a>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Your unit can arrive on the battlefield as if it were the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#2.-Reinforcements">Reinforcements step</a> of your Movement phase, and if every model in that unit has the <span class="tooltip00042" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00042"><span class="tt kwbu">Deep</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Strike</span></span> ability, you can set that unit up as described in the Deep Strike ability (even though it is not your Movement phase).<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>RESTRICTIONS:</b></span> You cannot use this Stratagem to enable a unit to arrive on the battlefield during a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#The-Battle-Round">battle round</a> it would not normally be able to do so in.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00044"><div class="tooltip_header">Invulnerable Saves</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Invulnerable-Saves"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Whether shielded by force fields, enveloped in mystical energies or simply possessed of preternatural senses and lightning-fast reflexes, some warriors are protected by more than mere physical armour.</p>Some models have an invulnerable save listed on their <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Datasheets-1">datasheet</a>. Each time an attack is allocated to a model with an invulnerable save, the controlling player must choose to use either that model’s Save characteristic or its invulnerable save, but not both. If a model has more than one invulnerable save, it can only use one of them – choose which it will use.<br><br>Unlike armour saving throws (which use a model’s Save characteristic), invulnerable saving throws are never modified by an attack’s Armour Penetration characteristic, but otherwise follow the normal rules for <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#4.-Saving-Throw">saving throws</a>.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Invulnerable Save:</b> Never modified by an attack’s AP.</li><li>The controlling player can choose to use either a model’s invulnerable save or its Save characteristic.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00045"><div class="tooltip_header">Benefit of Cover</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Benefit-of-Cover"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Shattered ruins and twisted wreckage afford much-needed shelter from enemy salvoes. Even heavily armoured warriors unfazed by small arms fire are thankful for such cover when foes bring their biggest guns to bear.</p>Models can sometimes gain a measure of protection from <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Terrain-Features">terrain features</a>. The rules <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Craters-and-Rubble">below</a> detail the conditions under which a terrain feature confers the Benefit of Cover on a model.<br><br>Each time a ranged attack is allocated to a model that has the Benefit of Cover, add 1 to the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#4.-Saving-Throw">saving throw</a> made for that attack (excluding <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Invulnerable-Saves">invulnerable saving throws</a>). Models with a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Datasheets-1">Save characteristic</a> of 3+ or better cannot have the Benefit of Cover against attacks with an Armour Penetration characteristic of 0. Multiple instances of the Benefit of Cover are not cumulative – a model cannot benefit from this rule more than once at any one time.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Benefit of Cover:</b> Add 1 to armour saving throws against ranged attacks.</li><li>Does not apply to models with a Save of 3+ or better against attacks with an AP of 0.</li><li>Multiple instances are not cumulative.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00046"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEnemy"><div class="str10Name">GO TO GROUND</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(10%) sepia(76%) saturate(6071%) hue-rotate(352deg) brightness(102%) contrast(99%);" ;="" class="str10Shooting str10ColorEnemy"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Core – Battle Tactic Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">Seeking salvation from incoming fire, warriors hurl themselves into whatever cover they can find.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Your opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One <span class="kwb">INFANTRY</span> unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Until the end of the phase, all models in your unit have a 6+ <span class="tooltip00044" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00044"><span class="tt kwbu">invulnerable</span> <span class="tt kwbu">save</span></span> and have the <span class="tooltip00045" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00045"><span class="tt kwbu">Benefit</span> <span class="tt kwbu">of</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Cover</span></span>.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00047"><div class="tooltip_header">Charge Bonus</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Charge-Bonus"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Many warriors thunder headlong into combat, using the impetus of their charge to bring swift death to their foes.</p>Each time a unit makes a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Charging-with-a-Unit">Charge move</a>, until the end of the turn, that unit has the <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#_col2-6">Fights First</a> ability.
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00048"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEnemy"><div class="str10Name">HEROIC INTERVENTION</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(10%) sepia(76%) saturate(6071%) hue-rotate(352deg) brightness(102%) contrast(99%);" ;="" class="str10Charge str10ColorEnemy"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Core – Strategic Ploy Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">Voices raised in furious war cries, your warriors surge forth to meet the enemy’s onslaught head-on.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Your opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#CHARGE-PHASE">Charge phase</a>, just after an enemy unit ends a <span class="tooltip00024" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00024"><span class="tt kwbu">Charge</span> <span class="tt kwbu">move</span></span>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One unit from your army that is within 6" of that enemy unit and would be eligible to declare a charge against that enemy unit if it were your Charge phase.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Your unit now declares a charge that targets only that enemy unit, and you resolve that charge as if it were your Charge phase.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>RESTRICTIONS:</b></span> You can only select a <span class="kwb">VEHICLE</span> unit from your army if it is a <span class="kwb">WALKER</span>. Note that even if this charge is successful, your unit does not receive any <span class="tooltip00047" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00047"><span class="tt kwbu">Charge</span> <span class="tt kwbu">bonus</span></span> this turn.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00049"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEither"><div class="str10Name">COUNTER-OFFENSIVE</div>

<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>WHEN:</b></span> <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#FIGHT-PHASE">Fight phase</a>, just after an enemy unit has fought.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One unit from your army that is within <span class="tooltip00031" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00031"><span class="tt kwbu">Engagement</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Range</span></span> of one or more enemy units and that has not already been selected to fight this phase.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Your unit fights next.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00050"><div class="tooltip_header">Precision</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Precision"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
<p class="ShowFluff legend2">Precision attacks can pick high-value targets out in a crowd, whether through the unerring aim of a sniper or the pinpoint accuracy of a blade-master’s strike.</p>Weapons with <span class="kwb2 bluefont">[PRECISION]</span> in their profile are known as Precision weapons. Each time an attack made with such a weapon successfully wounds an Attached unit (see <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Normal-Moves">Leader</a> ability), if a <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model in that unit is visible to the attacking model, the attacking model’s player can choose to have that attack allocated to that <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model instead of following the normal <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Making-Attacks">attack sequence</a>.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>When targeting an Attached unit, the attacking model’s player can have the attack allocated to a <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model in that unit visible to the bearer.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00051"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEither"><div class="str10Name">EPIC CHALLENGE</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(40%) sepia(12%) saturate(2210%) hue-rotate(121deg) brightness(94%) contrast(88%);" ;="" class="str10Fight str10ColorEither"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Core – Epic Deed Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">The legends of the 41st Millennium are replete with deadly duels between mighty champions.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>WHEN:</b></span> <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#FIGHT-PHASE">Fight phase</a>, when a <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> unit from your army that is within <span class="tooltip00031" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00031"><span class="tt kwbu">Engagement</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Range</span></span> of one or more <span class="tooltip00018" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00018"><span class="tt kwbu">Attached</span> <span class="tt kwbu">units</span></span> is selected to fight.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One <span class="kwb">CHARACTER</span> model in your unit.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Until the end of the phase, all melee attacks made by that model have the <span class="kwb2 bluefont"><span class="tooltip00050" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00050"><span class="tt kwbu">[PRECISION]</span></span></span> ability.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00052"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEnemy"><div class="str10Name">WEAVEWËRKE BUTTRESS</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(10%) sepia(76%) saturate(6071%) hue-rotate(352deg) brightness(102%) contrast(99%);" ;="" class="str10Shooting str10ColorEnemy"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Dêlve Assault Shift – Strategic Ploy Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">Every dêlve is threaded with force fields to shield against catastrophe, a protection that can be tapped in battle to support advances into hostile territory.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Your opponent’s <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets, if units from your army have <span class="tooltip00008" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00008"><span class="tt kwbu">Hostile</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Acquisition</span></span>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One <span class="tooltip00009" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00009" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00009"><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVNO"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVPP"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVDS"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVBO"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHA"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="" class="snFl LVHE"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVMO"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVVS"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVHS"><span class="kwb kwbu">LEAGUES</span> <span class="kwb2 kwbu">OF</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">VOTANN</span></span> <span class="kwb kwbu">INFANTRY</span></span> unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEnemy"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> Until the end of the phase, each time an attack targets your unit, subtract 1 from the <span class="tooltip00021" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00021"><span class="tt kwbu">Wound</span> <span class="tt kwbu">roll</span></span>.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00053"><div class="tooltip_header">Pile In</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#1.-Pile-In"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
When a unit Piles In, you can move each model in that unit that is not already in base-to-base contact with an enemy model up to 3" – this is a Pile-in move. For a Pile In to be possible, a unit must be able to end these moves within <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range">Engagement Range</a> of one or more enemy units and in <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Unit-Coherency">Unit Coherency</a>. If these conditions cannot be met, no models in the unit can make Pile-in moves this phase and you progress to making melee attacks with that unit. Otherwise, the unit can make Pile-in moves.<br><br>Each time a model makes a Pile-in move, it must end that move closer to the closest enemy model. If it can also end that move in base-to-base contact with one or more enemy models while still satisfying all of the conditions above, it must do so. The controlling player chooses the order in which to move their models.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Pile-in Move:</b> Up to 3".</li><li>Every model that moves must end closer to the closest enemy model, and in base-to-base contact with an enemy model if possible. The unit must end in Unit Coherency and within Engagement Range of at least one enemy unit (or no models can Pile In).</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00054"><div class="tooltip_header">Consolidate</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#3.-Consolidate"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
After a unit has finished making all of its melee attacks, it Consolidates. Each time a unit Consolidates, you can move each model in that unit that is not already in base-to-base contact with an enemy model up to 3" – this is a Consolidation move. For a Consolidation to be possible, a unit must be able to end these moves within <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Engagement-Range">Engagement Range</a> of one or more enemy units and in <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Unit-Coherency">Unit Coherency</a>. If these conditions cannot be met, then each model in that unit can instead make a Consolidation move towards the closest <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Objective-Markers">objective marker</a>, but only if, after doing so, that unit is within range of that objective marker and in Unit Coherency. If these conditions also cannot be met, no models in the unit can make Consolidation moves this phase and that unit’s fight ends.<br><br>If a unit can end its Consolidation within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, then each time one of its models makes a Consolidation move, it must end that move closer to the closest enemy model. If it can also end that move in base-to-base contact with one or more enemy models while still satisfying all of the conditions above, it must do so. The controlling player chooses the order in which to move their models.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li><b>Consolidation Move:</b> Up to 3".</li><li>Every model that moves must end closer to the closest enemy model, and in base-to-base contact with an enemy model if possible. The unit must end in Unit Coherency and within Engagement Range of at least one enemy unit if possible.</li><li>If the above is not possible, each model can move towards the closest objective marker, but this must result in the unit being within range of it and in Unit Coherency.</li><li>If the above is also not possible, no models can Consolidate.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00055"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEither"><div class="str10Name">UNSTOPPABLE FORCE</div>
<div class="str10Border"><div class="str10DiamondWrap"><div><div class="str10Diamond"><div style="filter: invert(40%) sepia(12%) saturate(2210%) hue-rotate(121deg) brightness(94%) contrast(88%);" ;="" class="str10Fight str10ColorEither"></div></div></div><div class="str10Pos2"><div class="str10Diamond"><div class="str10CP">1CP</div></div></div></div>
<div class="str10Type">Dêlve Assault Shift – Strategic Ploy Stratagem</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>
<div class="str10Legend ShowFluff">With their enemies within reach, the Kin’s fury and momentum become an elemental force that will not be stayed.</div>
<div class="str10Line ShowFluff"></div>
<div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>WHEN:</b></span> <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#FIGHT-PHASE">Fight phase</a>.<br><br><span class="str10ColorEither"><b>TARGET:</b></span> One <span class="tooltip00007" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00007" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00007"><span style="display:none;" <div class="str10Text"><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>WHEN:</b></span> Your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#SHOOTING-PHASE">Shooting phase</a>, just after a <span class="tooltip00060" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00060" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00060"><span class="kwb kwbu">BRÔKHYR</span></span>, <span class="tooltip00061" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00061" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00061"><span class="kwb kwbu">IRONKIN</span> <span class="kwb kwbu">STEELJACKS</span></span> or <a class="kwbOne" href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Arkanyst-Evaluator"><span class="kwb kwbo">ARKANYST</span> <span class="kwb kwbo">EVALUATOR</span></a> unit from your army has shot.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>TARGET:</b></span> That <span class="kwb">BRÔKHYR</span>, <span class="kwb">IRONKIN</span> <span class="kwb">STEELJACKS</span> or <span class="kwb">ARKANYST</span> <span class="kwb">EVALUATOR</span> unit.<br><br><span class="str10ColorYour"><b>EFFECT:</b></span> If your unit <span class="tooltip00063" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00063"><span class="tt kwbu">Remained</span> <span class="tt kwbu">Stationary</span></span> in your <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#MOVEMENT-PHASE">Movement phase</a> this turn, it can make a <span class="tooltip00039" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00039"><span class="tt kwbu">Normal</span> <span class="tt kwbu">move</span></span>.</div>
<div class="str10Line"></div>

If you spent YP during this usage of this Stratagem, you can re-roll the Hit roll and re-roll a Wound roll of 1 instead.</div>

</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00069"><p>The <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Transports"><span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span></a> keyword is used in the following Leagues of Votann datasheets:</p><span class="contents"><div style="clear:both; margin-bottom:8px;"><div class="BatRole">Dedicated Transports</div><div class="BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Kapricus-Carrier" class="contentColor">Kapricus Carrier</a></div></div><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Sagitaur" class="contentColor">Sagitaur</a></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div><div style="clear:both; margin-bottom:8px;"><div class="BatRole">Other</div><div class="BreakInsideAvoid"><div class="clFl LVLV LVHE LVNO LVPP LVDS LVBO LVHA LVMO" style="padding: 1px 10px 1px 15px;"><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/datasheets.html#Hekaton-Land-Fortress" class="contentColor">Hekaton Land Fortress</a></div></div><div style="clear:both"></div></div></span></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00070"><div class="tooltip_header">Embark</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Embark"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div>
If a unit makes a <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Normal-Moves">Normal</a>, <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Advance-Moves">Advance</a> or <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Fall-Back-Moves">Fall Back move</a>, and every model in that unit ends that move within 3" of a friendly <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Transports"><span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span></a> model, they can embark within it. A unit cannot embark if it has already <a href="/wh40k10ed/the-rules/core-rules/#Disembark">disembarked</a> from a <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model in the same phase. Remove the unit from the battlefield and place it to one side – it is now embarked within that <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> model. Unless otherwise stated, units cannot do anything or be affected in any way while they are embarked.<br><br><div class="BreakInsideAvoid frameLight Corner14" style=""><ul class="redSquare"><li>A unit can embark within a friendly <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span> if all of its models end a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move within 3" of that <span class="kwb">TRANSPORT</span>.</li><li>A unit cannot embark and disembark in the same phase.</li></ul></div>
</span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00071"><div class=" str10Wrap BreakInsideAvoid str10ColorEnemy"><div class="str10Name">NEW HORIZONS</div>

</div></div></span></div><div class="tooltip_templates"><span id="tooltip_content00082"><div class="tooltip_header">Assailed From Every Angle</div><a href="/wh40k10ed/factions/leagues-of-votann/#Assailed-From-Every-Angle"><div class="tooltip_link"></div></a><div style="clear:both"></div><div class="detachName">Persecution Prospect Detachment</div><p class="ShowFluff legend2">The Kin of persecution prospects are skilled in escalating their attacks with unrelenting volleys of fire. They pressure the kindred’s foes repeatedly from infiltration positions, unleash enfilading fire from the flanks and surge forward to blaze away at point-blank range before manoeuvring swiftly to other attack angles. Besides the death and destruction they cause, the prospect’s forces leave foes disarrayed, with little chance to rally any ravaged survivors before other Kin capitalise on their plight.</p><span class="tooltip00007" data-tooltip-content="#tooltip_content00007" data-tooltip-anchor="#tooltip_content00007"><span style="display:none;" class="snFl LVLV"><span class="kwb">LEAGUES</span> <span
